lines.english: These shaven-headed saints have ruined my house.
My daughter-in-law was first called Dhannia, the woman of wealth,
Aasaa:
but now she is called Raam-jannia, the servant of the Lord. ||1||
Says Kabeer, listen, O mother:
They have caused my son to start chanting the Lord's Name. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਹੁ ਰਹੁ ਰੀ ਬਹੁਰੀਆ ਘੂੰਘਟੁ ਜਿਨਿ ਕਾਢੈ ॥
lines.translit: rahu rahu ree bahureea ghoonghatt jin kaadtai |
lines.english: Stay, stay, O daughter-in-law - do not cover your face with a veil.
Aasaa:
these shaven-headed saints have done away with my low social status. ||2||3||33||

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਨ ਕੀ ਗੈਲਿ ਤੋਹਿ ਜਿਨਿ ਲਾਗੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: aun kee gail tohi jin laagai |1|
lines.english: do not follow in her footsteps. ||1||
The one before you used to veil her face;

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤ ਕੀ ਬਾਰ ਲਹੈਗੀ ਨ ਆਢੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: ant kee baar lahaigee na aadtai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: In the end, this shall not bring you even half a shell. ||1||Pause||
Your veil shall be true only if

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਿਨ ਦਸ ਪਾਂਚ ਬਹੂ ਭਲੇ ਆਈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: din das paanch bahoo bhale aaee |2|
lines.english: that for a few days, people will say, "What a noble bride has come". ||2||
The only merit in veiling your face is
only if she passes her life singing the Lord's Praises. ||4||1||34||
Says Kabeer, the soul-bride shall win,

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਇ ਕੂਦਹਿ ਅਰੁ ਨਾਚੈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: har gun gaae koodeh ar naachai |3|
lines.english: you skip, dance and sing the Glorious Praises of the Lord. ||3||
Aasaa:
I would rather be cut apart by a saw, than have You turn Your back on me.
I am a sacrifice to You - please, turn Your face to me, O Beloved Lord.
Hug me close, and listen to my prayer. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਉ ਤਨੁ ਚੀਰਹਿ ਅੰਗੁ ਨ ਮੋਰਉ ॥
lines.translit: jau tan cheereh ang na morau |
lines.english: Even if You cut my body apart, I shall not pull my limbs away from You.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਵਟੁ ਦੇ ਮੋ ਕਉ ਕਾਹੇ ਕਉ ਮਾਰੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: karavatt de mo kau kaahe kau maare |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Why have You turned Your back to me? Why have You killed me? ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਮ ਤੁਮ ਬੀਚੁ ਭਇਓ ਨਹੀ ਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: ham tum beech bheo nahee koee |
lines.english: Between You and I, there is no other.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਮਹਿ ਸੁ ਕੰਤ ਨਾਰਿ ਹਮ ਸੋਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: tumeh su kant naar ham soee |3|
lines.english: You are the Husband Lord, and I am the soul-bride. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਿੰਡੁ ਪਰੈ ਤਉ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਨ ਤੋਰਉ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: pindd parai tau preet na torau |2|
lines.english: Even if my body falls, I shall not break my bonds of love with You. ||2||
now, I place no reliance in you. ||4||2||35||
Aasaa:
Says Kabeer, listen, O people:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਰੀ ਕੋ ਕਾਹੂ ਮਰਮੁ ਨ ਜਾਨਾਂ ॥
lines.translit: koree ko kaahoo maram na jaanaan |
lines.english: No one knows the secret of God, the Cosmic Weaver.
you shall know that the whole world is only a small piece of His woven fabric. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਬ ਤੁਮ ਸੁਨਿ ਲੇ ਬੇਦ ਪੁਰਾਨਾਂ ॥
lines.translit: jab tum sun le bed puraanaan |
lines.english: When you listen to the Vedas and the Puraanas,

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੁ ਜਗੁ ਆਨਿ ਤਨਾਇਓ ਤਾਨਾਂ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sabh jag aan tanaaeo taanaan |1| rahaau |
lines.english: He has stretched out the fabric of the whole world. ||1||Pause||
He has made the earth and sky His loom.
Upon it, He moves the two bobbins of the sun and the moon. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋਲਾਹੇ ਘਰੁ ਅਪਨਾ ਚੀਨੑਾਂ ਘਟ ਹੀ ਰਾਮੁ ਪਛਾਨਾਂ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jolaahe ghar apanaa cheenaan ghatt hee raam pachhaanaan |3|
lines.english: I have come to understand my own home, and recognize the Lord within my heart. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਈ ਜੋਰਿ ਬਾਤ ਇਕ ਕੀਨੀ ਤਹ ਤਾਂਤੀ ਮਨੁ ਮਾਨਾਂ ॥
lines.translit: paaee jor baat ik keenee teh taantee man maanaan |
lines.english: Placing my feet together, I have accomplished one thing - my mind is pleased with that Weaver.
Says Kabeer, when my body workshop breaks,
the Weaver shall blend my thread with His thread. ||4||3||36||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਮੈਲੁ ਜੇ ਤੀਰਥ ਨਾਵੈ ਤਿਸੁ ਬੈਕੁੰਠ ਨ ਜਾਨਾਂ ॥
lines.translit: antar mail je teerath naavai tis baikuntth na jaanaan |
lines.english: With filth within the heart, even if one bathes at sacred places of pilgrimage, still, he shall not go to heaven.
Aasaa:

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੋਕ ਪਤੀਣੇ ਕਛੂ ਨ ਹੋਵੈ ਨਾਹੀ ਰਾਮੁ ਅਯਾਨਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: lok pateene kachhoo na hovai naahee raam ayaanaa |1|
lines.english: Nothing is gained by trying to please others - the Lord cannot be fooled. ||1||
Worship the One Divine Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚਾ ਨਾਵਣੁ ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਸੇਵਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: saachaa naavan gur kee sevaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The true cleansing bath is service to the Guru. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੈਸੇ ਮੇਂਡੁਕ ਤੈਸੇ ਓਇ ਨਰ ਫਿਰਿ ਫਿਰਿ ਜੋਨੀ ਆਵਹਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jaise mendduk taise oe nar fir fir jonee aaveh |2|
lines.english: As is the frog, so is that mortal; he is reincarnated, over and over again. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਲ ਕੈ ਮਜਨਿ ਜੇ ਗਤਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਨਿਤ ਨਿਤ ਮੇਂਡੁਕ ਨਾਵਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: jal kai majan je gat hovai nit nit mendduk naaveh |
lines.english: If salvation can be obtained by bathing in water, then what about the frog, which is always bathing in water?

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਹੁ ਕਠੋਰੁ ਮਰੈ ਬਾਨਾਰਸਿ ਨਰਕੁ ਨ ਬਾਂਚਿਆ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: manahu katthor marai baanaaras narak na baanchiaa jaaee |
lines.english: If the hard-hearted sinner dies in Benaares, he cannot escape hell.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਸੰਤੁ ਮਰੈ ਹਾੜੰਬੈ ਤ ਸਗਲੀ ਸੈਨ ਤਰਾਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: har kaa sant marai haarranbai ta sagalee sain taraaee |3|
lines.english: And even if the Lord's Saint dies in the cursed land of Haramba, still, he saves all his family. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਿਨਸੁ ਨ ਰੈਨਿ ਬੇਦੁ ਨਹੀ ਸਾਸਤ੍ਰ ਤਹਾ ਬਸੈ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: dinas na rain bed nahee saasatr tahaa basai nirankaaraa |
lines.english: Where there is neither day nor night, and neither Vedas nor Shaastras, there, the Formless Lord abides.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਕਬੀਰ ਨਰ ਤਿਸਹਿ ਧਿਆਵਹੁ ਬਾਵਰਿਆ ਸੰਸਾਰਾ ॥੪॥੪॥੩੭॥
lines.translit: keh kabeer nar tiseh dhiaavahu baavariaa sansaaraa |4|4|37|
lines.english: Says Kabeer, meditate on Him, O mad-men of the world. ||4||4||37||
